content.full_text:
Lucille Kring
1619 W. Lorane Way
Anaheim, California 92802-3917
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-97-476
Dear Ms. Kring:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
You were a candidate for the Anaheim City Council in November 1996. Anaheim has a
local ordinance with a limit of $1,000 per contributor for any city election. You were
unsuccessful in the election; you have not declared to run for another office.
You have a
campaign debt that you would like to retire.
QUESTIONS
1.
Does Proposition 208 apply to you since you are neither an officeholder nor a candidate
for afuture election?
2.
Some supporters have already contributed the $1,000 maximum contribution allowed by
the local ordinance. Does Proposition 208 allow them to contribute an additional $250?
3.
Is the $250 added onto the $1,000 contribution, and does it therefore violate the local
ordinance?
Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
RAvI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION
September25, 1997
428 1 STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No. A-97-476
Page 2
CONCLUSIONS
1. You retain your status as a candidate until such time as you terminate your status and
your committee. (Sections 82007, 84214 and Regulation 18404, copy enclosed.) Since you still
have a campaign committee which has debt, you are considered a candidate. The provisions of
Proposition 208, which became effective on January 1, 1997, apply to you.
2. We have advised that contributions made in connection with an election held prior to
January 1, 1997, are not aggregated with the same person’s contributions made after January 1,
1997, to pay down a candidate’s debt for that election. (Johnson Advice Letter, No. A-96-316a,
Sutton Advice Letter No. 1-97-079). Therefore, persons who have already contributed at or more
than the applicable Proposition 208 limit to a candidate prior to January 1, 1997, may contribute
again up to the Proposition 208 limit to pay down the candidate’s debt after January 1, 1997.
Section 85305(c) provides that funds may be collected at any time to retire debt for any
election held prior to January 1, 1997, provided that the funds are collected pursuant to the
contribution limits specified in Article 3, Chapter 5 of Proposition 208. (Ramirez Advice Letter,
No. A-97-262, Miller Advice Letter, No. A-97-350, Gould Advice Letter, No. A-96-336, and
Weiner Advice Letter, No. A-96-330.) Pursuant to Section 85301(b), assuming this is the
applicable limit, in a district of 100,000 or more residents, a person may contribute $250 to a
candidate for each election the candidate is on the ballot to retire pre-1997 debt for an election
held before January 1, 1997. However, we are not advising as to whether the local ordinance
permits an additional $250 contribution from a person who already contributed $1,000 to your
campaign committee.
3. The Anaheim ordinance limits contributions to $1,000 per election.
2 Although state
law does not prohibit a contributor who had given the maximum contribution under the local
ordinance from making an additional contribution of $250 to repay the debt, the additional
contribution may violate the local ordinance.
3 Therefore, we advise you to consult your city
attorney or city clerk regarding the local ordinance so you do not violate local law by accepting
an over-the-limit contribution for the 1996 debt repayment. (Sutton Advice Letter, supra.)
2 We are not advising whether this local ordinance is valid or not valid under Proposition 208. (See
Sections 81013, 85400(c) and 85706.)
Please note that we advised in the Ramirez Advice Letter, No. I-97-079a, that where a local limit is no
longer in force, it does not matter whether a contributor already gave the maximum permitted under the old local
law for the 1996 election or not. The contributor may still give the additional contribution allowed under Section
85301.


File No. A-97-476
Page 3
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Jill Stecher
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:JS:tls
Enclosure
I:\Advice Ltrs\97476.wpd
